title: Write a GitHub Actions Workflow
description:
## Define a CI Pipeline

GitHub Actions workflows are YAML files describing **jobs** made of **steps**.

**Your task:** write a workflow at **`/root/ci.yml`** that:
1. triggers on **`push`**
2. defines a job named **`build`** that runs on **`ubuntu-latest`**
3. the `build` job has a step that runs **`echo building`**

> 💡 The structure is `on:` → `jobs:` → `<job>:` → `runs-on:` + `steps:`. Validate the YAML with `python3 -c "import yaml; yaml.safe_load(open('/root/ci.yml'))"`.

hints.title: Workflow skeleton
hints.body: Declare the trigger, then a jobs map; each job sets runs-on and a list of steps.
hints.command:
printf 'on: push
jobs:
  build:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - run: echo building
' > /root/ci.yml

validation.description: Checks that /root/ci.yml defines a 'build' job on ubuntu-latest.

commands.command: python3 -c "import yaml; d=yaml.safe_load(open('/root/ci.yml')); print('ok' if isinstance(d,dict) and 'jobs' in d and 'build' in d['jobs'] else 'no')"
